info.name: Content Broadcasting System
info.description: REST API for managing and broadcasting digital content across display devices in an educational institution. Teachers upload content; a principal reviews, approves, and schedules it into display slots.

request.description: Creates a new teacher account. The `role` field only accepts `"teacher"`. Principal accounts cannot be created through this endpoint; they are provisioned via `npm run seed`.

response.name: Account created. Returns auth bundle with access token and sets cookie.

description.content: Two cookies are set: `refresh_token` (HttpOnly, SameSite=Strict, scoped to /api/auth) and `XSRF-TOKEN` (readable by JS, used for CSRF).

request.description: Authenticates a user and returns an access token plus sets refresh and CSRF cookies.

Issues a new access token using the `refresh_token` HttpOnly cookie. Implements refresh token rotation — the old token is revoked and a new one is issued. Reuse of an already-revoked token revokes the entire token family.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: apikey
Added as a part of security scheme: apikey

request.description: Revokes the current refresh token and clears auth cookies.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

item.name: Get live content for a teacher (public)

variable.description: Numeric ID of the teacher whose live content to fetch.

request.description:
Public endpoint — no authentication required. Returns the single currently-active content item per subject for the given teacher, applying time-window filtering and deterministic round-robin rotation.

**Eligibility rules** — a content item is live when:

  - `status = approved`

  - both `start_time` and `end_time` are set (content without a time
    window is never shown)

  - `NOW()` falls within `[start_time, end_time]`


**Rotation algorithm** — for each subject the eligible items are ordered by `id ASC`. A deterministic position is computed as:

  ```
  totalCycle = Σ rotation_duration_minutes  (default 5 per item)
  position   = floor(Unix time in minutes) % totalCycle
  ```

The item whose duration bracket contains `position` is returned. The cycle loops continuously with no shared state, so any server replica returns the same answer for the same clock value.

**Edge cases** — all return HTTP 200 with an empty `live` object and `message: "No content available"` rather than a 4xx:

  - unknown or non-teacher `teacherId`

  - no approved content for that teacher

  - approved content exists but is outside its time window

  - invalid (non-integer) `teacherId`

response.name: Live content by subject. An empty `live` object (with optional `message`) means nothing is currently active — this is never a 4xx error.

variable.description: Numeric ID of the teacher whose live content to fetch.

formdata.description: Image file. Allowed types: image/jpeg, image/png, image/gif. Max 10 MB.
ISO 8601. Content is only active on or after this time.
ISO 8601. Must be after start_time. Content stops being active after this time.
Suggested display duration per rotation cycle.

request.description: Uploads an image file to S3 and creates a content record with status `pending`. Only teachers can upload. The file is validated by MIME type (jpg/png/gif) and size (max 10 MB).

response.name: Content uploaded and record created.

request.description: Creates a new display slot for the given subject. Subject is normalised to lowercase. Duplicate subjects return 409.

header.description: Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer
Added as a part of security scheme: bearer

request.description:
Returns the **single currently-active content item per slot**, determined by a stateless clock-based rotation. No authentication required — intended for display devices (kiosks, TVs) that poll this endpoint to know what to show right now.

**Rotation algorithm** — for each slot the scheduled items are walked in `rotation_order ASC`. The active item is determined by:

```
  totalCycle = Σ duration_minutes across all items in the slot
  position   = floor(Unix time in minutes) % totalCycle

 ```

The item whose `duration_minutes` bracket contains `position` is returned. The cycle loops continuously with no shared state.

**Eligibility** — an item is included in the pool only when:

- `status = approved`
    
- both `start_time` and `end_time` are set on the content row
    
- `NOW()` falls within `[start_time, end_time]`
    

Each `file_url` is a pre-signed S3 URL valid for 1 hour; devices must re-poll before expiry to refresh it.

response.name: Currently active content keyed by slot ID. A slot key is absent when none of its items are within their time window.

variable.description: ID of the content item to unschedule.

request.description: Removes the schedule entry for the given content item.
